meaning: adv.慢性地,习惯性地,长期地
examples: Most of them were chronically ill.
It is required by law to shut down banks which it regards as chronically short of capital.
Chronically high levels of these hormones have been linked to heart disease.
They're composed of high-energy, highly penetrating particles, and astronauts would be exposed to them chronically.
In their experience, they believe 50% of healthcare costs for chronically ill can be avoided.
